title: Kosavalasu Bhagavan Temple
tagline: Kosavalasu Bhagavan Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Bhagavan Kovil is a Vishnu temple located near Dharapuram of Tirupur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
overview.description: Bhagavan Kovil is a Vishnu temple located near Dharapuram of Tirupur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a Vaishnavite temple dedicated to Lord ThirumalaiSamy. This temple is best known for the Ugadi festival which falls on Indian summer months of March or April and precisely on the Telugu new year day called Ugadi. This temple gets visitors in lakhs during the world-famous festival.

topPlaces.name: Kolathupalayam
topPlaces.description: Kolathupalayam is a panchayat town in Dharapuram taluk of Tiruppur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is one of the 15 panchayat towns in the district.

topPlaces.name: Peramiyam
topPlaces.description: Peramium or Peramiyam is a village and a panchayat near Dharapuram and Mulanur in Tirupur district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kosavalasu Bhagavan Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kosavalasu Bhagavan Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kosavalasu Bhagavan Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Coimbatore International Airport (74 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (80 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kosavalasu Bhagavan Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kosavalasu Bhagavan Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kosavalasu Bhagavan Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kosavalasu Bhagavan Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kosavalasu Bhagavan Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nallathangal Dam, Thirumalaigoundenvalasu, Kolathupalayam, Peramiyam.
